burden.mortalityRate: Low with care; crises can be fatal

riskFactors: female sex (early onset) / male sex (late onset)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical fatigable weakness with antibody testing and electrophysiology; evaluate thymus

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; limited access to specialized testing and crisis management in many areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Antibody testing and SFEMG concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Pyridostigmine, steroids, immunosuppressants; IVIG/plasmapheresis for crisis where available; thymectomy in selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Cost and access barriers for crisis therapies and long-term immunosuppression monitoring
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing tertiary neurology and ICU capacity in major cities
